title: cyperus flavescens subsp. microglumis
intro: Cyperus flavescens subsp. microglumis, also known as the small-flower umbrella sedge or umbrella sedge, is a type of perennial plant in the sedge family, native to tropical Africa and Asia. This plant has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antibacterial, and antifungal activities. The health benefits associated with Cyperus flavescens subsp. microglumis include the treatment of wounds, skin conditions, and respiratory problems, as well as its potential to reduce fever and alleviate digestive issues. Herbal preparations of this plant can be made into teas, infusions, or ointments, and are often used in combination with other herbs to enhance their medicinal effects.
